title: T-116 — Seed: i18n namespace 02-settings.json (PL + EN, §14.2)
prompt:
# T-116 — Seed: i18n namespace 02-settings.json (PL + EN, §14.2)

PRD: 02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §14.2 (i18n)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create the next-intl translation namespace files `02-settings.json` for both PL and EN locales (Phase 1 MVP languages per §14.2). File contains the static UI strings consumed by SETTINGS module screens (T-041..T-115 page titles, table headers, action labels, validation messages, modal titles). Per-row dynamic labels remain in `reference_schemas.presentation_json.label_<lang>` (out of scope here).

## Implementation contract
1. Create `apps/web/messages/pl/02-settings.json` and `apps/web/messages/en/02-settings.json` mirroring identical key structure.
2. Top-level keys grouped by sub-area: `nav`, `users`, `roles`, `infra`, `schema`, `rules`, `reference`, `tenant`, `modules`, `flags`, `d365`, `email`, `notifications`, `security`, `onboarding`, `audit`, `validations` (matches sub-modules a..e).
3. EN is the canonical source per §14.2 fallback rule; PL is a complete translation (no missing keys).
4. Add a vitest that loads both files and asserts (a) identical key structure across PL and EN, (b) no empty string values, (c) no key references stale module names from out-of-scope sub-modules.
5. No code edits to existing screens (those tasks already reference next-intl keys; this task only seeds the bundles).

## Files
- apps/web/messages/en/02-settings.json [create]
- apps/web/messages/pl/02-settings.json [create]
- apps/web/messages/__tests__/02-settings.namespace.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` array.

## Test strategy
1. RED: write the namespace test asserting key parity + non-empty + presence of expected top-level groups.
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/messages/__tests__/02-settings.namespace.test.ts`
2. RED must fail before bundle files exist.
3. GREEN: emit both bundles with full key set.
4. Re-run RED + `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not seed UK/RO/DE/FR (Phase 2/3 per §14.2 — explicitly out of scope per coverage.md gap row).
- Do not embed dynamic data in keys (use ICU placeholders only).
- Do not duplicate per-table dynamic labels here (those live in reference_schemas).

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + next-intl. PRD: 02-SETTINGS-PRD.md v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.description: Seed next-intl namespace `02-settings.json` for PL + EN with full key parity for all SETTINGS screens.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §14.2 mandates next-intl with namespace per moduł, Phase 1 MVP = PL+EN, EN fallback always available. The bundle aggregates all static UI strings referenced by T-041..T-115. Per-row dynamic field labels stay in `reference_schemas.presentation_json.label_<lang>` (NOT here). Phase 2/3 languages (UK/RO/DE/FR) explicitly out of scope per coverage.md gap row §14.2 Phase 2/3 i18n.
out_of_scope: UK / RO / DE / FR translations (Phase 2/3 per §14.2)
Per-row dynamic field labels (live in reference_schemas)
acceptance_criteria: Given the namespace test runs, when both bundles are loaded, then `flatten(en)` and `flatten(pl)` produce identical sorted key lists (zero diff).
Given the EN bundle loads, when iterated, then no value is the empty string and every key matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*(\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)*$`.

Given the bundle is loaded, when top-level groups are listed, then it includes all of: nav, users, roles, infra, schema, rules, reference, tenant, modules, flags, d365, email, notifications, security, onboarding, audit, validations.
Given the PL bundle loads, when key 'validations.v_set_60' is read, then the message references `{parentLevel}` as an ICU placeholder (parameterized, not interpolated).
test_strategy: RED: write namespace test asserting parity + regex + groups + ICU placeholder.
GREEN: emit both JSON bundles with full key set.
risk_red_lines: Do not embed dynamic data in keys (use ICU placeholders only).
Do not duplicate per-table dynamic labels here (those live in reference_schemas).
